message:
chore(beads): close 5qln, file 4upy + nz2v parity bugs

Closes frankenredis-5qln (AUTH 2-arg WRONGPASS — fixed in 5c693d8).

Files:
  - frankenredis-4upy: CLIENT LIST output diverges from upstream Redis 7.2
    (missing argv-mem/multi-mem/rbs/rbp/redir; flag/lib field reorder).
  - frankenredis-nz2v: GEOPOS uses 16-digit f64 Display vs upstream's
    %.17Lg shortest-form.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

+{"id":"frankenredis-4upy","title":"fr-runtime: CLIENT LIST output diverges from upstream Redis 7.2 (missing argv-mem/multi-mem/rbs/rbp/redir, extra watch, flags position)","description":"fr-runtime's CLIENT LIST output diverges from upstream Redis 7.2 in several ways: (1) MISSING fields: argv-mem (input argv memory), multi-mem (queued MULTI memory), rbs (read-buffer size), rbp (read-buffer position), redir (CLIENT TRACKING REDIRECT target id, default -1). (2) EXTRA field: watch=N (count of WATCHed keys) — Redis 7.2 dropped this field. (3) FIELD ORDER: fr emits flags=N at the very end; Redis 7.2 emits flags right after idle, before db. (4) lib-name / lib-ver order: Redis emits these AFTER resp; fr emits before. (5) fd is hardcoded to 0 vs Redis's actual epoll fd — minor but a real divergence; tools that grep on fd=N may misbehave.

Acceptance: a fresh fr-server with one connected client emits CLIENT LIST in a single line whose tokens (and order) match upstream Redis 7.2's CLIENT LIST output for an equivalent client, modulo the fd field (fd=0 stays since fr doesn't track real fds for clients) and the per-instance values (id, addr port, qbuf, qbuf-free, tot-mem). Tools like redis_exporter that parse the line on field names (regex) will match. The conformance harness's CLIENT LIST live-oracle test gains a side-by-side text diff that asserts identical token ordering.

Reference: ran 'CLIENT LIST' against fr (port 16500) and vendored redis 7.2.4 (port 16501) with the same shape of single connected redis-cli client. fr line: 'id=2 addr=... laddr=... fd=0 name= age=0 idle=0 db=0 sub=0 psub=0 ssub=0 multi=-1 watch=0 qbuf=0 qbuf-free=0 obl=0 oll=0 omem=0 tot-mem=0 events=r cmd=client|list user=default lib-name= lib-ver= resp=2 flags=N'. Redis line: 'id=3 addr=... laddr=... fd=7 name= age=0 idle=0 flags=N db=0 sub=0 psub=0 ssub=0 multi=-1 qbuf=26 qbuf-free=20448 argv-mem=10 multi-mem=0 rbs=16384 rbp=16384 obl=0 oll=0 omem=0 tot-mem=37786 events=r cmd=client|list user=default redir=-1 resp=2 lib-name= lib-ver='.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-29T22:08:17.262396364Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-29T22:08:17.262396364Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id": "frankenredis-4x9a", "title": "redis parity: runtime EVAL BGREWRITEAOF regression should expect NOSCRIPT", "description": "Full fr-runtime runs are still red on eval_bgrewriteaof_rewrites_aof_snapshot because command parity now rejects BGREWRITEAOF from Lua with NOSCRIPT, but the runtime regression still expects the old success reply. Update the runtime expectation to the Redis-aligned NOSCRIPT surface and verify through rch.", "status": "closed", "priority": 2, "issue_type": "task", "created_at": "2026-04-21T18:50:31.754862465Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "updated_at": "2026-04-21T18:54:47.075307402Z", "closed_at": "2026-04-21T18:54:47.072900897Z", "close_reason": "Completed", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0}

Add explicit sanitize mode state plus GETUSER, ACL LIST, and ACL SAVE parity for the two flags.", "status": "closed", "priority": 2, "issue_type": "task", "created_at": "2026-04-21T09:11:24.199344491Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "updated_at": "2026-04-21T09:20:38.175078835Z", "closed_at": "2026-04-21T09:20:38.172833161Z", "close_reason": "Completed", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0}

Apply a focused Redis-compatible subset from redis.conf at startup, starting with bind/port/auth/replica connection directives, and freeze behavior with tests.", "status": "closed", "priority": 1, "issue_type": "task", "created_at": "2026-04-18T01:40:27.553100702Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "updated_at": "2026-04-18T01:49:17.142171541Z", "closed_at": "2026-04-18T01:49:17.140300951Z", "close_reason": "Applied basic redis.conf startup directives and added binary startup coverage", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0}
 {"id": "frankenredis-5pnv", "title": "HIGH: promote fr-conformance live-oracle tests from #[ignore] to self-spawning CI gate", "description": "## Summary

`crates/fr-conformance/src/lib.rs` already carries a rich live-diff harness (`run_live_redis_diff`, `run_live_redis_protocol_diff`, `run_live_redis_diff_for_cases`) that exercises our `Runtime` side-by-side with a real `redis-server` and captures mismatches into a structured report. Every actual entry point is gated behind `#[ignore = \"requires running redis-server on localhost:6379\"]` — so in practice these tests never run in `cargo test --workspace`, and divergences creep in invisibly.

At least four suites are dark:
- `live_redis_core_errors_matches_runtime` (lib.rs:7418)
- `live_redis_core_replication_stable_matches_runtime` (lib.rs:7431)
- `live_redis_fr_p2c_006_replication_journey_matches_runtime` (lib.rs:7455)
- `live_redis_protocol_negative_matches_runtime` (lib.rs:7469)

The vendored oracle is already in-tree at `legacy_redis_code/redis/src/redis-server`, so the excuse ("needs an external server") no longer applies.

Both `frankenredis-v4x3` (ACL arity wording) and `frankenredis-j4vq` (FUNCTION STATS nil/int flip-flop) would have been caught instantly by this gate.

## Applying /testing-conformance-harnesses

This is Pattern 6 (Process-Based) wrapped around Pattern 1 (Differential):
- **Harness owns the process.** Add `LiveOracleConfig::spawn_vendored()` that `Command::new()`s `legacy_redis_code/redis/src/redis-server`, picks a free port, writes a tmp dir, and returns a `Drop`-guarded handle that `SHUTDOWN NOSAVE`s and reaps on drop.
- **Remove `#[ignore]` from the four live tests.** They should now always run. If the binary isn't present (or fails to start, e.g. on the rch remote worker that doesn't vendor it), fall back to `Skipped { reason: ... }` via the ConformanceTest trait per the skill, NOT a silent pass.
- **Emit the compliance matrix.** After the run, dump a `artifacts/live_oracle_report_<ts>.json` (schema already exists as `DIFFERENTIAL_REPORT_SCHEMA_VERSION`) and a human-readable Markdown table scoring each fixture (passed / failed / xfail).
- **XFAIL, not SKIP.** Known divergences (e.g. current RESP3 gaps, hash-field-TTL absence) land in `fr-conformance/DISCREPANCIES.md` with a bead reference; the harness marks them `ExpectedFailure` so they stay visible.

## Non-goals

- Do not yet drive the full `core_*.json` corpus (60+ fixtures) under the live gate — that's a follow-up once the process-spawning layer is reliable. Start with the four already-written ignored tests.
- Do not require the full oracle to be built inside rch workers; the gate can opportunistically skip on workers that don't have the binary while still failing locally in CI.

## Acceptance

- `cargo test -p fr-conformance` on a machine with the vendored oracle compiled fails if the runtime diverges from live Redis on any of the four suites.
- On a machine without the oracle, each live test reports `Skipped { reason: \"vendored redis-server not built\" }` and the workspace build stays green.
- A new `artifacts/live_oracle_report_*.json` is written per run and is schema-stable (versioned under `DIFFERENTIAL_REPORT_SCHEMA_VERSION`).
- First run exposes current divergences as explicit `ExpectedFailure` entries so they're triaged, not hidden.

## Labels

fr-conformance · conformance · testing-conformance-harnesses · live-oracle · ci", "status": "closed", "priority": 1, "issue_type": "feature", "created_at": "2026-04-23T03:27:54.751532516Z", "created_by": "cc-redis", "updated_at": "2026-04-23T17:23:42.154432030Z", "closed_at": "2026-04-23T17:23:42.152077474Z", "close_reason": "Fixed in 8318b5f — VendoredRedis spawner promotes the four live_redis_* tests from #[ignore] to auto-skip-on-missing-binary, with FR_CONFORMANCE_LIVE_ORACLE_STRICT=1 opt-in for the full mismatch gate.", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0, "labels": ["ci", "conformance", "fr-conformance", "live-oracle", "testing-conformance-harnesses"]}
 {"id": "frankenredis-5pt6", "title": "BZPOPMAX wrongtype errors should return immediately", "status": "closed", "priority": 2, "issue_type": "bug", "created_at": "2026-04-10T21:37:44.122793728Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "updated_at": "2026-04-10T21:39:20.748396508Z", "closed_at": "2026-04-10T21:39:20.748062044Z", "close_reason": "BZPOPMAX now returns wrongtype errors immediately", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0}
+{"id":"frankenredis-5qln","title":"fr-runtime: AUTH 2-arg form returns NotConfigured error instead of WRONGPASS when user is unknown","description":"AUTH 2-argument form (AUTH <user> <pass>) returns 'ERR AUTH <password> called without any password configured for the default user...' when no requirepass is set, regardless of which user was named. Upstream Redis 7.2 returns 'WRONGPASS invalid username-password pair or user is disabled.' for the 2-arg form when the user doesn't exist (or fails password check). The 1-arg form (AUTH <pass>) correctly returns the NotConfigured error.

Root cause: fr-runtime::authenticate_user short-circuits on auth_state.auth_required() returning false → AuthFailure::NotConfigured, before checking if the named user actually exists in the ACL. handle_auth_command then surfaces AUTH_NOT_CONFIGURED_ERROR for both forms.

Reference: against vendored redis 7.2.4 with no requirepass, AUTH baduser password returns WRONGPASS. fr returns the NotConfigured error. Caught by an end-to-end command-by-command diff smoke that compared fr (port 16510) vs vendored redis (port 16511) on AUTH baduser password.

Acceptance: AUTH <user> <pass> returns 'WRONGPASS invalid username-password pair or user is disabled.' when:
  • the named user doesn't exist in the ACL (no requirepass configured), OR
  • the user exists but is disabled, OR
  • the password is wrong.
AUTH <pass> (1-arg form) keeps the NotConfigured error when the default user has nopass + no requirepass set. This matches upstream server.c::authCommand which only emits the NotConfigured error for the 1-arg path.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-29T22:10:19.727953429Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-29T22:16:22.763639629Z","closed_at":"2026-04-29T22:16:22.761528981Z","close_reason":"AUTH 2-arg form now returns WRONGPASS for unknown users / wrong passwords regardless of requirepass; 1-arg form preserves the upstream NotConfigured error. Pinned by two new fr-runtime regression tests. Shipped in 5c693d8.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

Run canonical rustfmt and validate the gate.", "status": "closed", "priority": 2, "issue_type": "bug", "assignee": "codex-rustfmt-gate", "created_at": "2026-04-25T00:41:20.746703497Z", "created_by": "codex-rustfmt-gate", "updated_at": "2026-04-25T00:43:39.156320503Z", "closed_at": "2026-04-25T00:43:39.154470461Z", "close_reason": "Completed in 4b7ab34", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0}
 {"id": "frankenredis-nw8l", "title": "redis parity: ACL command/category grants should not lose implicit key access", "description": "The current fr-runtime full test run is red on acl_case_insensitive_command_matching, acl_allcommands_resets_granular_permissions, acl_category_based_permissions, acl_deny_category_overrides_allow_all, acl_deny_command_wins_over_allow_all, acl_per_command_allow_specific_commands, acl_per_command_deny_specific_commands, and acl_per_command_override_category_deny. The common failure mode is command/category ACL grants unexpectedly denying access to key 'k', which suggests recent ACL key-pattern/default handling regressed Redis's implicit allkeys behavior for these rule sets. Repair the runtime ACL permission evaluation so command/category grants preserve the expected key access semantics, and verify with the focused fr-runtime tests plus crate-level checks via rch.", "status": "closed", "priority": 2, "issue_type": "task", "created_at": "2026-04-21T19:10:30.452167624Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "updated_at": "2026-04-21T19:13:44.389992065Z", "closed_at": "2026-04-21T19:13:44.387316901Z", "close_reason": "Completed", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0}
 {"id": "frankenredis-nwgw", "title": "Move SLOWLOG state to Store so dispatch_argv path returns real data", "description": "# Move SLOWLOG state to Store for Lua/dispatch_argv compatibility

## Problem
SLOWLOG GET/LEN/RESET in fr-command (the dispatch_argv path) returns empty arrays/0/OK because the slow log lives in Runtime's ServerState. Lua scripts calling redis.call(\"SLOWLOG\", \"GET\") get nothing.

## Design
Move the slowlog data structure from ServerState to Store:
- pub slowlog: VecDeque<SlowlogEntry>
- pub slowlog_id_counter: u64
- pub slowlog_log_slower_than_us: i64
- pub slowlog_max_len: usize

## Implementation
1. Define SlowlogEntry in fr-store (or move from fr-runtime):
   ```rust
   pub struct SlowlogEntry {
       pub id: u64,
       pub timestamp_sec: u64,
       pub duration_us: u64,
       pub argv: Vec<Vec<u8>>,
   }
   ```
2. Add the fields to Store
3. Add Store methods: record_slowlog(), get_slowlog(), slowlog_len(), reset_slowlog()
4. Update fr-command's slowlog_cmd to accept &mut Store and call these methods
5. Update fr-runtime to call store.record_slowlog() instead of server.record_slowlog()
6. Remove duplicate SlowlogEntry from runtime (or re-export from store)

## Why Store instead of Runtime
- Store is accessible from both dispatch_argv (Lua scripts, AOF replay) and the runtime
- The slow log is essentially per-database state, making Store the natural home
- This follows the same pattern we used for CONFIG GET/SET

## Migration path
- The runtime already has record_slowlog wired into execute_frame — just redirect to store
- slowlog_log_slower_than_us and slowlog_max_len are already CONFIG SET-able in runtime — redirect to store fields

## Testing
- Conformance: Lua script calls redis.call(\"SLOWLOG\", \"GET\") and gets real entries
- Unit test: record a slow command, verify SLOWLOG LEN returns 1

## Files to modify
- crates/fr-store/src/lib.rs (SlowlogEntry struct, Store fields, methods)
- crates/fr-command/src/lib.rs (slowlog_cmd accepts Store, reads real data)
- crates/fr-runtime/src/lib.rs (redirect slowlog recording to store)", "status": "closed", "priority": 1, "issue_type": "task", "created_at": "2026-04-03T00:20:25.774798027Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "updated_at": "2026-04-03T14:12:03.227702446Z", "closed_at": "2026-04-03T14:12:03.227428628Z", "close_reason": "Completed", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0}
+{"id":"frankenredis-nz2v","title":"fr-command: GEOPOS coordinate format diverges from upstream %.17Lg (16 vs 17 digits)","description":"GEOPOS emits coordinate doubles using Rust's f64 Display (Ryu shortest round-trip, typically 16 significant digits) where upstream Redis emits via addReplyHumanLongDouble formatted with %.17Lg (17 significant digits). Result: fr returns 0.9999999403953552 / 2.000000185646549 where Redis returns 0.99999994039535522 / 2.0000001856465488 for the same encoded geohash score. The actual lat/lng resolution is identical (same bit pattern decodes to the same point on the geoid), but the wire format differs in the trailing digit count.

Impact: low. Geo precision is dominated by the geohash bit budget (52 bits, ~0.6m at the equator), so the 17th digit is below noise. But this is a real wire-format divergence that breaks byte-equal diff tests.

Acceptance: GEOPOS / GEOSEARCH WITHCOORD format coordinate doubles with 17 significant digits in shortest-form (matches upstream %.17Lg). One reasonable Rust path: add a fr-command::format_redis_double(value: f64) -> String helper using a custom snprintf-style formatter, or use the printf crate. Conformance core_geo cases that currently XFAIL on GEOPOS lose the XFAIL.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-29T22:08:31.295252959Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-29T22:08:31.295252959Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id": "frankenredis-nzhr", "title": "redis parity: DEBUG needs NOSCRIPT guard and RESP error surface", "description": "Upstream metadata marks DEBUG as NOSCRIPT in legacy_redis_code/redis/src/commands/debug.json, but debug_cmd has no script_nesting_level guard at crates/fr-command/src/lib.rs:12794. Separately, the unknown-subcommand parity tests at crates/fr-command/src/lib.rs:25774 and :25788 expect a RESP Error frame, while debug_cmd currently returns CommandError::Custom at crates/fr-command/src/lib.rs:12913. Bring DEBUG into line with Redis script restrictions and reply semantics.", "status": "closed", "priority": 2, "issue_type": "task", "created_at": "2026-04-21T02:39:59.248821257Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "updated_at": "2026-04-21T03:13:03.803235604Z", "closed_at": "2026-04-21T03:13:03.800889203Z", "close_reason": "Completed", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0}

Sort list responses by instance name and add a deterministic golden.", "status": "closed", "priority": 2, "issue_type": "bug", "assignee": "cod_1", "created_at": "2026-04-28T20:50:41.132596051Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "updated_at": "2026-04-29T00:42:18.093810442Z", "closed_at": "2026-04-29T00:42:18.091884630Z", "close_reason": "Completed: SENTINEL list replies now sort by instance name and golden coverage freezes deterministic masters output.", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0, "labels": ["fr-sentinel", "golden"]}
 {"id": "frankenredis-o0zu", "title": "Expand stable core_migrate live Redis coverage", "description": "Add a stable core_migrate live differential subset against vendored Redis covering deterministic argument-validation and NOKEY behavior only, without overlapping the FUNCTION/SCRIPT lane or broadening runtime parity work.", "status": "closed", "priority": 2, "issue_type": "task", "created_at": "2026-04-18T22:51:37.300654297Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "updated_at": "2026-04-18T22:58:35.494182069Z", "closed_at": "2026-04-18T22:58:35.492298441Z", "close_reason": "Completed", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0}
